title: "Cameron & Trivedi -- Ch. 13: Bootstrap methods (via StatEcon)"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"

  enabled: true

---

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon
const DATA = first(filter(d -> isdir(d) && (isfile(joinpath(d, "auto.dta")) || isdir(joinpath(d, "musr"))), ["../../data", "data", raw"C:/EC/GitHub/StatEcon/data"]))
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using FixedEffectModels, StatsModels, GLM
using FixedEffectModels: Vcov
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using Optim, ForwardDiff
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, pdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq
_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x
```

The canonical estimators used in this chapter now live in `StatEcon` and are
called directly below:

  - `stata_probit`  — canonical probit (`src/stata_probit.jl`)
  - `stata_bsample` — Stata's `bsample` (`src/stata_bsample.jl`)
  - `stata_poisson`, `stata_nbreg`, `stata_test`, `stata_regress`,
    `stata_summarize`, `stata_ivregress_2sls`

The **bootstrap demonstrations** are the point of this chapter, so they are
kept inline as pedagogical scaffolding: the resampling engines
(`obs_bootstrap`, `panel_bootstrap`), the Stata-format printers
(`bootstrap_b_table_stata`), the `vce(bootstrap)` / `vce(jackknife)` wrappers
(`poisson_boot_stata`, `poisson_boot_ci_stata`, `poisson_boot_b_se_stata`,
`poisson_jackknife_stata`), the percentile-t pipeline (`bootstrap_tstar_stata`,
`percentilet_pvalue_stata`, `percentilet_ci_stata`, `mybootstrap_stata`), the
alternative resampling schemes (`bootparametric_nb_stata`,
`bootresidual_ols_stata`, `bootwild_ols_stata`), and the Heckman two-step
(`heckman_twostep_stata`).

```{julia}
#| output: false
# ── Bootstrap dataset loader ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Stata's musr/bootdata.dta is the first 50 obs of year02==1 from the
# doctor-visits data, keeping docvis chronic age. We rebuild it from
# mus10data so every cell is self-contained (no intermediate .dta needed).
function _c13_bootdata()
    d = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus10data.dta"))
    d = DataFrames.filter(r -> r.year02 == 1, d)[1:50, [:docvis, :chronic, :age]]
    for c in DataFrames.names(d)
        d[!, c] = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, c]))
    end
    return d
end

# ── Resampling engines ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
"""
    panel_bootstrap(f, df, idvar; B=400, seed=10101)

Cluster bootstrap on the panel identifier `idvar`. Applies `f(df_resampled)`
on each resample and returns a `B × k` matrix (one row per replicate). Each
sampled cluster gets a fresh unique id so panel estimators do not merge
duplicates (matching Stata's `bootstrap, cluster()`).
"""
function panel_bootstrap(f::Function, df, idvar::Symbol; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101,
                         verbose::Bool=true)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    ids = unique(df[!, idvar])
    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(df[!, idvar])
        push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
    end
    results = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    n_fail = 0
    for b in 1:B
        boot_ids = StatsBase.sample(rng, ids, length(ids); replace=true)
        row_idx = Int[]; new_id = Int[]
        for (k, i) in enumerate(boot_ids)
            rows = id_to_rows[i]
            append!(row_idx, rows)
            append!(new_id, fill(k, length(rows)))
        end
        sub = df[row_idx, :]
        sub[!, idvar] = new_id
        try
            push!(results, f(sub))
        catch
            n_fail += 1
        end
    end
    verbose && n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$B resamples failed (skipped)."
    isempty(results) && error("All bootstrap resamples failed.")
    return reduce(hcat, results)'
end

"""
    obs_bootstrap(f, df; B=400, seed=10101, verbose=true)

Observation-level bootstrap — resamples n rows with replacement (no cluster
structure). Matches Stata's `vce(bootstrap)` with no cluster specified.
"""
function obs_bootstrap(f::Function, df; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101, verbose::Bool=true)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    n = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    results = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    n_fail = 0
    for b in 1:B
        row_idx = StatsBase.sample(rng, 1:n, n; replace=true)
        sub = df[row_idx, :]
        try
            push!(results, f(sub))
        catch
            n_fail += 1
        end
    end
    verbose && n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$B resamples failed."
    isempty(results) && error("All bootstrap resamples failed.")
    return reduce(hcat, results)'
end

# ── Stata `%9.0g` sign-aware formatter shared by the printers below ──────
function _c13_g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
    (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
    sig_use = sig
    s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
    cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
    while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
        sig_use -= 1
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
    end
    0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
    lpad(s, w)
end
function _c13_commafmt(num)
    s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
    while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
    (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
end

# ── Minimal Stata-format `regress` printer (kept inline; the exported
# `stata_regress` uses a different display path and has no `noheader`) ───
function regress_stata(model; level::Float64=0.95, noheader::Bool=false)
    β  = StatsBase.coef(model); V = StatsBase.vcov(model)
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    nm_raw = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    nm = replace.(nm_raw, "(Intercept)" => "_cons")
    has_int = any(==("_cons"), nm)
    n = Int(StatsBase.nobs(model)); rss = StatsBase.deviance(model)
    r2 = StatsBase.r2(model); ar2 = StatsBase.adjr2(model)
    dof_r = Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(model))
    k = length(β) - (has_int ? 1 : 0)
    tss = rss / max(1 - r2, eps()); mss = tss - rss
    rmse = sqrt(rss / dof_r)
    Fstat = (mss / k) / (rss / dof_r)
    pF = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(k, dof_r), Fstat)
    yname = try string(StatsBase.responsename(model))
            catch; hasproperty(model, :yname) ? string(model.yname) : "y" end
    t_stat = β ./ se
    tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    p_val  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), abs.(t_stat)))
    ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se; ci_hi = β .+ tcrit .* se
    _g(x, w; sig=7) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end
    if !noheader
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s %10s %10s    %-13s = %10d\n",
                       "Source", "SS", "df", "MS", "Number of obs", n)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^34, "   ",
                Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %10.2f", "F($k, $dof_r)", Fstat))
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                       "Model", _g(mss,10), k, _g(mss/k,10), "Prob > F", pF)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                       "Residual", _g(rss,10), dof_r, _g(rss/dof_r,10), "R-squared", r2)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^34, "   ",
                Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %10.4f", "Adj R-squared", ar2))
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                       "Total", _g(tss,10), n-1, _g(tss/(n-1),10), "Root MSE", rmse)
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   yname, "Coefficient", "Std. err.", "t", "P>|t|", round(Int,100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for i in eachindex(β)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       nm[i], _g(β[i],10), _g(se[i],9), t_stat[i], p_val[i],
                       _g(ci_lo[i],11), _g(ci_hi[i],11))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    return nothing
end

function regress_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, y, xs;
                       level::Float64=0.95, vcov=nothing, weights=nothing,
                       noheader::Bool=false)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = xs isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ? [Symbol(xs)] : [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    f = StatsModels.term(ys) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(xsv))
    kw = weights === nothing ? (;) : (; weights=Symbol(weights))
    m = vcov === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f; kw...) :
                           FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f, vcov; kw...)
    regress_stata(m; level=level, noheader=noheader)
    return nothing
end

# ── Wald chi² test on a bootstrap (β, V, coefnames) vector (Stata `test`
# after `bootstrap _b:`; the exported `stata_test` only takes a model) ───
function _c13_test_boot(vars; β, V, coefnames, depvar::AbstractString="",
                        values=nothing)
    names = string.(coefnames)
    vs = vars isa AbstractVector ? string.(vars) : [string(vars)]
    vals = values === nothing ? zeros(length(vs)) : collect(values)
    p_idx(v) = (i = findfirst(==(string(v)), names);
                i === nothing && error("'$(v)' not in coefnames=$(names)"); i)
    q = length(vs); k = length(β); R = zeros(q, k); r = vals; labels = String[]
    for (row, v) in enumerate(vs)
        R[row, p_idx(v)] = 1.0
        prefix = isempty(depvar) ? "" : "[$depvar]"
        push!(labels, "$prefix$v = " * Printf.@sprintf("%g", vals[row]))
    end
    Rβ_r = R * collect(β) .- r
    chi2 = (Rβ_r' * (LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(R * Matrix(V) * R') \ Rβ_r))::Float64
    df = size(R, 1)
    pval = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(df), chi2)
    println()
    for (k_, lab) in enumerate(labels)
        Printf.@printf(" ( %d)  %s\n", k_, lab)
    end
    Printf.@printf("\n           chi2(%3d) = %7.2f\n", df, chi2)
    Printf.@printf("         Prob > chi2 = %9.4f\n", pval)
    return (; chi2, df, p = pval)
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: false
# ── `poisson ..., vce(bootstrap)` / `bootstrap _b: poisson` ─────────────
"""
    poisson_boot_stata(df, formula; B=400, seed=10101, level=0.95,
                       cluster_var=nothing, noheader=false, quiet=false)

Stata `poisson <depvar> <regs>, vce(boot, reps(B) seed(s))`. Fits Poisson,
then runs B observation-level (or cluster, via `cluster_var`) bootstrap
replicates to estimate Var(β̂). Prints the Stata bootstrap table with
"Observed coefficient | Bootstrap std. err. | z | P>|z| | Normal-based CI".
"""
function poisson_boot_stata(df, formula; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101,
                            level::Float64=0.95, quiet::Bool=false,
                            cluster_var=nothing, noheader::Bool=false)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    cols   = cluster_var === nothing ? needed : vcat(needed, [Symbol(cluster_var)])
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, cols])
    m   = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    β̂   = GLM.coef(m); cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n   = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)); k = length(β̂)
    ll  = GLM.loglikelihood(m)

    fml_null = StatsModels.term(Symbol(formula.lhs)) ~ StatsModels.term(1)
    m_null   = GLM.glm(fml_null, dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    pseudo_r2 = 1 - ll / GLM.loglikelihood(m_null)

    n_clusters = cluster_var === nothing ? 0 :
                 length(unique(dfc[!, Symbol(cluster_var)]))
    β_boot = if cluster_var === nothing
        obs_bootstrap(dfc; B = B, seed = seed, verbose = false) do d
            GLM.coef(GLM.glm(formula, d, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink()))
        end
    else
        panel_bootstrap(dfc, Symbol(cluster_var); B = B, seed = seed,
                        verbose = false) do d
            GLM.coef(GLM.glm(formula, d, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink()))
        end
    end
    V  = Statistics.cov(β_boot)
    se = sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V))

    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx     = setdiff(1:k, [intercept_idx])
    βs = β̂[slope_idx]; Vs = V[slope_idx, slope_idx]
    Wald   = (βs' * (LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(Vs) \ βs))::Float64
    Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(slope_idx)), Wald)

    z      = β̂ ./ se
    pv     = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    crit   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo  = β̂ .- crit .* se; ci_hi = β̂ .+ crit .* se

    if !quiet
        if !noheader
            println()
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-13s = %6s\n",
                           "Poisson regression", "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n))
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6s\n", "", "Replications", _c13_commafmt(B))
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.2f\n", "",
                           "Wald chi2($(length(slope_idx)))", Wald)
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.4f\n", "", "Prob > chi2", Wald_p)
            ll_str = Printf.@sprintf("Log likelihood = %.5f", ll)
            r2_str = Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %6.4f", "Pseudo R2", pseudo_r2)
            pad    = max(0, 78 - length(ll_str) - length(r2_str))
            println(ll_str, " "^pad, r2_str)
            println()
            if cluster_var !== nothing
                cl_str = Printf.@sprintf("(Replications based on %d clusters in %s)",
                                         n_clusters, cluster_var)
                println(lpad(cl_str, 78))
            end
        end
        println("-"^78)
        println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         ",
                "Normal-based")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(formula.lhs), 100*level)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                          vcat(slope_idx, [intercept_idx])
        for i in ord
            label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           label, _c13_g9(β̂[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z[i]),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", pv[i]),
                           _c13_g9(ci_lo[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
        end
        println("-"^78)
    end
    return (; β = β̂, se, V, β_boot, model = m, n, B)
end

"""
    poisson_boot_ci_stata(df, formula; B=999, seed=10101, level=0.95, quiet=false)

Stata `poisson, vce(boot, bca); estat bootstrap, all`. Computes all four
bootstrap CI types (Normal, Percentile, Bias-corrected, BCa) per coefficient
(BCa needs a leave-one-out jackknife pass for the acceleration `a`).
"""
function poisson_boot_ci_stata(df, formula; B::Int=999, seed::Int=10101,
                               level::Float64=0.95, quiet::Bool=false)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    m   = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    β̂   = GLM.coef(m); cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n   = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)); k = length(β̂)

    β_boot = obs_bootstrap(dfc; B = B, seed = seed, verbose = false) do d
        GLM.coef(GLM.glm(formula, d, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink()))
    end
    se_b = sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(Statistics.cov(β_boot)))
    bias = vec(Statistics.mean(β_boot, dims = 1)) .- β̂

    β_jack = zeros(n, k)
    for i in 1:n
        sub = dfc[setdiff(1:n, i), :]
        β_jack[i, :] = GLM.coef(GLM.glm(formula, sub,
                                        Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink()))
    end

    α   = 1 - level
    zα2 = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - α/2)
    cis = Vector{Vector{NTuple{2, Float64}}}(undef, k)
    z0_v = zeros(k); a_v = zeros(k)
    for j in 1:k
        b̂  = β̂[j]; bj = β_boot[:, j]
        N_lo = b̂ - zα2 * se_b[j]; N_hi = b̂ + zα2 * se_b[j]
        P_lo = Statistics.quantile(bj, α/2); P_hi = Statistics.quantile(bj, 1 - α/2)
        p_lt = sum(bj .< b̂) / B
        p_lt_safe = clamp(p_lt, 1/(2B), 1 - 1/(2B))
        z0 = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), p_lt_safe)
        BC_lo = Statistics.quantile(bj, Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), 2*z0 - zα2))
        BC_hi = Statistics.quantile(bj, Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), 2*z0 + zα2))
        bjk = β_jack[:, j]; b̄jk = Statistics.mean(bjk); d = b̄jk .- bjk
        a   = sum(d .^ 3) / (6 * (sum(d .^ 2)) ^ 1.5)
        BCa_lo = Statistics.quantile(bj, Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(),
                        z0 + (z0 - zα2) / (1 - a*(z0 - zα2))))
        BCa_hi = Statistics.quantile(bj, Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(),
                        z0 + (z0 + zα2) / (1 - a*(z0 + zα2))))
        cis[j] = [(N_lo, N_hi), (P_lo, P_hi), (BC_lo, BC_hi), (BCa_lo, BCa_hi)]
        z0_v[j] = z0; a_v[j] = a
    end

    g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=8) = begin
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 11 : 10
        sig_use = sig
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1; s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w)
    end

    if !quiet
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                       "Poisson regression", "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n))
        Printf.@printf("%48s%-18s= %10s\n", "", "Replications", _c13_commafmt(B))
        println()
        intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
        slope_idx     = setdiff(1:k, [intercept_idx])
        ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                          vcat(slope_idx, [intercept_idx])
        println("-"^78)
        println("             |    Observed               Bootstrap")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient       Bias    std. err.  [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(formula.lhs), 100*level)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        labels = ["(N)", "(P)", "(BC)", "(BCa)"]
        for i in ord
            label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
            for r in 1:4
                lo, hi = cis[i][r]
                if r == 1
                    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s   %s  %4s\n",
                                   label, g9(β̂[i]; w=10), g9(bias[i]; w=8),
                                   g9(se_b[i]; w=9), g9(lo; w=9), g9(hi; w=8), labels[r])
                else
                    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s   %s  %4s\n",
                                   "", " "^10, " "^8, " "^9,
                                   g9(lo; w=9), g9(hi; w=8), labels[r])
                end
            end
        end
        println("-"^78)
        println("Key:   N: Normal")
        println("       P: Percentile")
        println("      BC: Bias-corrected")
        println("     BCa: Bias-corrected and accelerated")
    end
    return (; β = β̂, bias, se = se_b, β_boot, β_jack, cis, z0 = z0_v, a = a_v)
end

"""
    poisson_boot_b_se_stata(df, formula; B=400, seed=10101, level=0.95,
                            vce=:oim, quiet=false)

Stata `bootstrap _b _se, reps(B) seed(s): poisson`. On each resample saves
BOTH β̂ and se(β̂) (OIM or robust via `vce`), so the bootstrap distribution
covers the SE-of-SE. Prints a two-block table (`docvis` / `docvis_se`).
"""
function poisson_boot_b_se_stata(df, formula; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101,
                                 level::Float64=0.95, quiet::Bool=false,
                                 vce::Symbol=:oim)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    m   = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    β̂   = GLM.coef(m); cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n   = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)); k = length(β̂)

    function _se_of(model)
        if vce == :robust
            yv  = Float64.(GLM.response(model)); μ = GLM.predict(model)
            Xm  = GLM.modelmatrix(model); n_b = size(Xm, 1)
            H   = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ) * Xm
            S   = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal((yv .- μ).^2) * Xm
            Hi  = LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(H)))
            V_  = Hi * S * Hi * (n_b / (n_b - 1))
            return sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_))
        else
            return sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(Matrix(GLM.vcov(model))))
        end
    end
    se_obs = _se_of(m)

    bs = obs_bootstrap(dfc; B = B, seed = seed, verbose = false) do d
        m_b = GLM.glm(formula, d, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
        vcat(GLM.coef(m_b), _se_of(m_b))
    end
    β_boot   = bs[:, 1:k]; se_boot = bs[:, (k + 1):(2k)]
    boot_se_β  = vec(Statistics.std(β_boot,  dims = 1))
    boot_se_se = vec(Statistics.std(se_boot, dims = 1))

    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1 - level)/2)
    function _z_p_ci(obs_v, boot_se_v)
        z = obs_v ./ boot_se_v
        p = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
        return z, p, obs_v .- crit .* boot_se_v, obs_v .+ crit .* boot_se_v
    end
    z_β,  p_β,  lo_β,  hi_β  = _z_p_ci(β̂,     boot_se_β)
    z_se, p_se, lo_se, hi_se = _z_p_ci(se_obs, boot_se_se)

    if !quiet
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%-59s%-13s = %3d\n", "Bootstrap results", "Number of obs", n)
        Printf.@printf("%59s%-13s = %3d\n", "", "Replications", B)
        println()
        println("-"^78)
        println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         ",
                "Normal-based")
        println("             | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     ",
                "[", round(Int, 100*level), "% conf. interval]")
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        depvar = string(formula.lhs)
        intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
        slope_idx     = setdiff(1:k, [intercept_idx])
        ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                          vcat(slope_idx, [intercept_idx])
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", depvar)
        for i in ord
            label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           label, _c13_g9(β̂[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(boot_se_β[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_β[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_β[i]),
                           _c13_g9(lo_β[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_β[i]; w=10))
        end
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", depvar * "_se")
        for i in ord
            label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           label, _c13_g9(se_obs[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(boot_se_se[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_se[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_se[i]),
                           _c13_g9(lo_se[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_se[i]; w=10))
        end
        println("-"^78)
    end
    return (; β = β̂, se_obs, boot_se_β, boot_se_se, β_boot, se_boot, model = m, n, B)
end

"""
    bootstrap_b_table_stata(β_obs, β_boot, coefnames; n_obs, depvar="", level=0.95,
                            command="", expressions=String[])

Stata `bootstrap _b: <command>` output — a single flat block with
"Observed coefficient | Bootstrap std. err. | z | P>|z| | Normal-based CI".
"""
function bootstrap_b_table_stata(β_obs::AbstractVector, β_boot::AbstractMatrix,
                                 coefnames::AbstractVector;
                                 n_obs::Int, depvar::AbstractString = "",
                                 level::Float64 = 0.95,
                                 command::AbstractString = "",
                                 expressions::AbstractVector = String[])
    B    = size(β_boot, 1)
    se_b = vec(Statistics.std(β_boot, dims = 1))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    z    = β_obs ./ se_b
    pv   = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    lo   = β_obs .- crit .* se_b; hi = β_obs .+ crit .* se_b

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-59s%-13s = %3s\n",
                   "Bootstrap results", "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n_obs))
    Printf.@printf("%59s%-13s = %3s\n", "", "Replications", _c13_commafmt(B))
    if !isempty(command) || !isempty(expressions)
        println()
        isempty(command) || Printf.@printf("%14s%s\n", "Command:  ", command)
        for ex in expressions
            Printf.@printf("%14s%s\n", "$(ex.first):  ", ex.second)
        end
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   depvar, 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for (i, lab) in enumerate(coefnames)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       lab, _c13_g9(β_obs[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se_b[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", pv[i]),
                       _c13_g9(lo[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    return (; β = β_obs, se = se_b, z, p = pv, ci_lo = lo, ci_hi = hi, B, n_obs)
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: false
# ── Percentile-t pipeline (§13.5–13.6) ──────────────────────────────────
"""
    bootstrap_tstar_stata(df, formula, var; B=999, seed=10101, vce=:robust,
                          command="", save_csv=nothing, quiet=false)

Percentile-t bootstrap for a single Poisson coefficient. Fits
`stata_poisson(df, formula; vce)` to get θ = β̂_var and σ_θ, then bootstraps
`t* = (β̂*_var − θ)/SE*_var` over B resamples. Returns `(; β_orig, se_orig,
tstars, B, n)`.
"""
function bootstrap_tstar_stata(df, formula, var::Symbol;
                               B::Int=999, seed::Int=10101, vce::Symbol=:robust,
                               command::AbstractString="", save_csv=nothing,
                               quiet::Bool=false)
    fit0 = stata_poisson(df, formula; vce = vce, quiet = true)
    cn0  = fit0.coefnames_glm
    i0   = findfirst(==(string(var)), cn0)
    i0 === nothing && error("'$(var)' not in coefnames=$(cn0)")
    β_orig  = fit0.β_glm[i0]; se_orig = sqrt(fit0.V_glm[i0, i0])

    tstar_mat = obs_bootstrap(df; B = B, seed = seed, verbose = false) do d
        fit_b = stata_poisson(d, formula; vce = vce, quiet = true)
        cn_b  = fit_b.coefnames_glm
        i_b   = findfirst(==(string(var)), cn_b)
        i_b === nothing && error("'$(var)' not in resample coefnames")
        return [(fit_b.β_glm[i_b] - β_orig) / sqrt(fit_b.V_glm[i_b, i_b])]
    end
    tstars = vec(tstar_mat)

    if save_csv !== nothing
        mkpath(dirname(save_csv))
        CSV.write(save_csv, DataFrames.DataFrame(tstar = tstars))
    end
    if !quiet
        bootstrap_b_table_stata([0.0], reshape(tstars, :, 1), ["tstar"];
            n_obs = DataFrames.nrow(df), depvar = "", command = command,
            expressions = ["tstar" => "(_b[$(var)]-$(β_orig))/_se[$(var)]"])
    end
    return (; β_orig, se_orig, tstars, B, n = DataFrames.nrow(df))
end

"""
    percentilet_pvalue_stata(tstars, β_orig, se_orig; null=0.0, quiet=false)

Symmetric two-sided percentile-t Wald test:
`p = (1/B)·Σ 1{|t*_b| > |t_obs|}`, `t_obs = (β_orig − null)/se_orig`.
"""
function percentilet_pvalue_stata(tstars::AbstractVector, β_orig::Real, se_orig::Real;
                                  null::Real = 0.0, quiet::Bool = false)
    t_obs = (β_orig - null) / se_orig; B = length(tstars)
    p_val = sum(abs(t) > abs(t_obs) for t in tstars) / B
    quiet || @printf("p-value = %.7g\n", p_val)
    return (; t_obs, p_value = p_val, B)
end

"""
    percentilet_ci_stata(tstars, β_orig, se_orig; level=0.95, quiet=false)

Percentile-t CI: `[θ + q(α/2)·SE_θ, θ + q(1−α/2)·SE_θ]`.
"""
function percentilet_ci_stata(tstars::AbstractVector, β_orig::Real, se_orig::Real;
                              level::Real = 0.95, quiet::Bool = false)
    α = 1 - level; plo = α / 2; phi = 1 - α / 2
    q_lo = Statistics.quantile(tstars, plo); q_hi = Statistics.quantile(tstars, phi)
    lb = β_orig + q_lo * se_orig; ub = β_orig + q_hi * se_orig
    gd(x) = begin
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.7g", x)
        (0 < abs(x) < 1) && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        s
    end
    pct_str(p) = (q = p * 100; isinteger(q) ? string(Int(q)) :
                  rstrip(rstrip(Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", q), '0'), '.'))
    if !quiet
        @printf("%s and %s percentiles of t* distn: %s, %s\n",
                pct_str(plo), pct_str(phi), gd(q_lo), gd(q_hi))
        @printf("%g percent percentile-t confidence interval is  (%s,%s)\n",
                level * 100, gd(lb), gd(ub))
    end
    return (; q_lo, q_hi, lb, ub, level)
end

"""
    mybootstrap_stata(df, formula, var; B=999, seed=10101, vce=:robust,
                      command="", null=0.0, quiet=false)

One-shot percentile-t p-value (Stata's `mybootstrap` program): wraps
`bootstrap_tstar_stata` + `percentilet_pvalue_stata`.
"""
function mybootstrap_stata(df, formula, var::Symbol; B::Int=999, seed::Int=10101,
                           vce::Symbol=:robust, command::AbstractString="",
                           null::Real=0.0, quiet::Bool=false)
    res = bootstrap_tstar_stata(df, formula, var;
                                B=B, seed=seed, vce=vce, command=command, quiet=true)
    pv  = percentilet_pvalue_stata(res.tstars, res.β_orig, res.se_orig;
                                   null=null, quiet=quiet)
    return (; pvalue = pv.p_value, t_obs = pv.t_obs, B = pv.B,
              tstars = res.tstars, β_orig = res.β_orig, se_orig = res.se_orig)
end

# ── Jackknife (§13.8) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
"""
    poisson_jackknife_stata(df, formula; mse=true, level=0.95, quiet=false)

Stata `poisson ..., vce(jackknife, mse)`. Delete-1 jackknife SE:
`V_jack = (n-1)/n · Σ (β̂_(i) − c)(β̂_(i) − c)ᵀ`, `c = β̂` if `mse` else `β̄`.
Inference uses t with df = n − 1; omnibus stat `F(k, n − 1)`.
"""
function poisson_jackknife_stata(df, formula; mse::Bool = true,
                                 level::Float64 = 0.95, quiet::Bool = false)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        col = dfc[!, c]
        if eltype(col) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(col) === Float32
            dfc[!, c] = Float64.(col)
        end
    end
    fit  = stata_poisson(dfc, formula; vce = :oim, quiet = true)
    β_glm  = fit.β_glm; cn = fit.coefnames_glm
    n = fit.n; k = fit.k; ll = fit.ll
    ll_null = ll / (1 - fit.pseudo_r2); pseudo_r2 = fit.pseudo_r2

    β_jack = Matrix{Float64}(undef, n, k)
    rows = 1:DataFrames.nrow(dfc)
    for i in 1:n
        fit_i = stata_poisson(dfc[setdiff(rows, i), :], formula; vce = :oim, quiet = true)
        β_jack[i, :] = [fit_i.β_glm[findfirst(==(name), fit_i.coefnames_glm)] for name in cn]
    end

    centre = mse ? β_glm : vec(Statistics.mean(β_jack, dims = 1))
    devs   = β_jack .- centre'
    V_jack = ((n - 1) / n) * (devs' * devs)

    se   = sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_jack)); t = β_glm ./ se
    df_t = n - 1
    p    = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(df_t), abs.(t)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(df_t), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = β_glm .- crit .* se; ci_hi = β_glm .+ crit .* se

    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx = setdiff(1:k, [intercept_idx])
    F_val, F_p = NaN, NaN
    if !isempty(slope_idx)
        β_s = β_glm[slope_idx]; V_s = V_jack[slope_idx, slope_idx]
        Wald_s = β_s' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V_s) * β_s
        F_val  = Wald_s / length(slope_idx)
        F_p    = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(length(slope_idx), df_t), F_val)
    end

    if !quiet
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%-56s%-13s = %6s\n",
                       "Poisson regression", "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n))
        Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6s\n", "", "Replications", _c13_commafmt(n))
        if isfinite(F_val)
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.2f\n", "", "F($(length(slope_idx)), $df_t)", F_val)
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.4f\n", "", "Prob > F", F_p)
        end
        ll_str = Printf.@sprintf("Log likelihood = %.5f", ll)
        r2_str = Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %6.4f", "Pseudo R2", pseudo_r2)
        pad_h  = max(0, 78 - length(ll_str) - length(r2_str))
        println(ll_str, " "^pad_h, r2_str)
        println()
        println("-"^78)
        println("             |              Jackknife")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  std. err.       t    P>|t|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(formula.lhs), 100*level)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                          vcat(collect(slope_idx), [intercept_idx])
        for i in ord
            label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           label, _c13_g9(β_glm[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", t[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                           _c13_g9(ci_lo[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
        end
        println("-"^78)
    end
    ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? collect(slope_idx) :
                                      vcat(collect(slope_idx), [intercept_idx])
    cn_ord = intercept_idx === nothing ? cn[ord] : vcat(cn[slope_idx], "_cons")
    return (; β = β_glm[ord], V = V_jack[ord, ord], se = se[ord],
              t = t[ord], p = p[ord], ci_lo = ci_lo[ord], ci_hi = ci_hi[ord],
              β_jack, coefnames = cn_ord, n, k, F = F_val, F_p,
              ll, ll_null, pseudo_r2, model = fit.model)
end
```

```{julia}
#| output: false
# ── Alternative resampling schemes (§13.7) & Heckman (§13.4.5) ──────────
"""
    bootparametric_nb_stata(df, muhat, α, formula; seed=nothing, quiet=false)

Parametric NB2 bootstrap (§13.7.2): draws ν ~ Gamma(1/α, α),
y* ~ Poisson(μ̂·ν), overwrites `formula.lhs` in a copy of `df`, and refits
NB2 via `stata_nbreg`. Returns the `stata_nbreg` named tuple.
"""
function bootparametric_nb_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame,
                                 muhat::AbstractVector, α::Real, formula;
                                 seed::Union{Int, Nothing} = nothing, quiet::Bool = false)
    rng = seed === nothing ? Random.default_rng() : Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    n   = length(muhat)
    ν      = Distributions.rand(rng, Distributions.Gamma(1/α, α), n)
    y_star = [Distributions.rand(rng, Distributions.Poisson(m * v)) for (m, v) in zip(muhat, ν)]
    df_b = DataFrames.DataFrame(df)
    df_b[!, Symbol(formula.lhs)] = y_star
    return stata_nbreg(df_b, formula; quiet = quiet)
end

"""
    bootresidual_ols_stata(df, formula; seed=nothing, vcov=nothing, quiet=false)

Residual bootstrap for OLS with iid errors (§13.7.3): fits OLS, resamples
residuals û*_i, forms y*_i = x_iᵀβ̂ + û*_i, refits. Prints both `regress`
tables. Returns `(; orig, boot, uhat, idx, ystar)`.
"""
function bootresidual_ols_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, formula;
                                seed::Union{Int, Nothing} = nothing,
                                vcov = nothing, quiet::Bool = false)
    rng = seed === nothing ? Random.default_rng() : Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    m0  = vcov === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.reg(df, formula) :
                             FixedEffectModels.reg(df, formula, vcov)
    quiet || regress_stata(m0)
    quiet || println("(option xb assumed; fitted values)")
    yvar = Symbol(formula.lhs)
    yhat = FixedEffectModels.predict(m0, df)
    uhat = df[!, yvar] .- yhat
    n    = length(uhat)
    idx  = StatsBase.sample(rng, 1:n, n; replace = true)
    ystar = yhat .+ uhat[idx]
    df_b = DataFrames.DataFrame(df); df_b[!, yvar] = ystar
    m_b  = vcov === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.reg(df_b, formula) :
                              FixedEffectModels.reg(df_b, formula, vcov)
    quiet || regress_stata(m_b)
    return (; orig = m0, boot = m_b, uhat, idx, ystar)
end

"""
    bootwild_ols_stata(df, formula; seed=nothing, vcov=nothing, quiet=false)

Wild bootstrap for OLS (§13.7.4) with Mammen two-point weights
η ∈ {-0.618034, +1.618034}, P(b) ≈ 0.2764. Prints both `regress` tables and
the `(<n> real changes made)` line. Returns `(; orig, boot, η, ustar, ystar)`.
"""
function bootwild_ols_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, formula;
                            seed::Union{Int, Nothing} = nothing,
                            vcov = nothing, quiet::Bool = false)
    rng = seed === nothing ? Random.default_rng() : Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    m0  = vcov === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.reg(df, formula) :
                             FixedEffectModels.reg(df, formula, vcov)
    quiet || regress_stata(m0)
    quiet || println("(option xb assumed; fitted values)")
    yvar = Symbol(formula.lhs)
    yhat = FixedEffectModels.predict(m0, df)
    uhat = df[!, yvar] .- yhat
    n_   = length(uhat)
    a, b = -0.618034, 1.618034
    flip = [Random.rand(rng) > 0.723607 for _ in 1:n_]
    η    = ifelse.(flip, b, a)
    ustar = η .* uhat; ystar = yhat .+ ustar
    quiet || println("($(sum(flip)) real changes made)")
    df_b = DataFrames.DataFrame(df); df_b[!, yvar] = ystar
    m_b = vcov === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.reg(df_b, formula) :
                             FixedEffectModels.reg(df_b, formula, vcov)
    quiet || regress_stata(m_b)
    return (; orig = m0, boot = m_b, η, ustar, ystar)
end

"""
    heckman_twostep_stata(df, fml_outcome, fml_selection; depvar_y="lny",
                          depvar_d="dy", level=0.95, quiet=false)

Stata `heckman <y> <xs>, select(<d> = <zs>) twostep` — Heckman (1979)
two-step with first-stage-corrected variance. Prints the lny / dy / /mills
blocks with σ, ρ. Returns the coefficient/variance NamedTuple.
"""
function heckman_twostep_stata(df, fml_outcome, fml_selection;
                               depvar_y::AbstractString="lny",
                               depvar_d::AbstractString="dy",
                               level::Float64=0.95, quiet::Bool=false)
    sel_vars = StatsModels.termvars(fml_selection)
    out_vars = StatsModels.termvars(fml_outcome)
    needed   = unique(vcat(sel_vars, out_vars))
    df_h = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed], sel_vars)
    n_full = DataFrames.nrow(df_h)

    m_pr = GLM.glm(fml_selection, df_h, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
    γ̂   = GLM.coef(m_pr); cn_pr = GLM.coefnames(m_pr)
    Z   = GLM.modelmatrix(m_pr); xb_full = Z * γ̂
    λ_full  = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_full) ./
              Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_full)
    δ_full  = λ_full .* (λ_full .+ xb_full)

    d_full = Int.(df_h[!, Symbol(depvar_d)])
    is_pos = Bool[d == 1 for d in d_full]
    n_pos  = sum(is_pos); n_zero = n_full - n_pos

    df_pos = df_h[is_pos, :]; df_pos.invmills = λ_full[is_pos]
    out_xs = out_vars[2:end]
    X_pos  = hcat(ones(n_pos), [Float64.(df_pos[!, v]) for v in out_xs]..., df_pos.invmills)
    y_pos  = Float64.(df_pos[!, Symbol(depvar_y)])
    α̂      = X_pos \ y_pos; ε̂ = y_pos .- X_pos * α̂

    β̂_λ    = α̂[end]; δ_pos = δ_full[is_pos]
    σ̂²     = (ε̂'ε̂) / n_pos + β̂_λ^2 * Statistics.mean(δ_pos)
    σ̂      = sqrt(σ̂²); ρ̂ = β̂_λ / σ̂

    safe_sym_inv(A; rtol::Float64=1e-10) = begin
        As = LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(A)
        try LinearAlgebra.inv(As) catch; LinearAlgebra.pinv(Matrix(As); rtol=rtol) end
    end
    XX_inv = safe_sym_inv(X_pos' * X_pos)
    Φ_full = clamp.(Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_full), 1e-12, 1 - 1e-12)
    φ_full = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_full)
    w_full = (φ_full .^ 2) ./ (Φ_full .* (1 .- Φ_full))
    A_γ    = Z' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(w_full) * Z
    Vγ     = safe_sym_inv(A_γ); Z_pos = Z[is_pos, :]
    D_pos  = LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(δ_pos)
    M1     = X_pos' * (LinearAlgebra.I(n_pos) - ρ̂^2 .* D_pos) * X_pos
    M2     = ρ̂^2 .* (X_pos' * D_pos * Z_pos) * Vγ * (Z_pos' * D_pos * X_pos)
    V_α    = σ̂² .* XX_inv * (M1 + M2) * XX_inv
    se_α   = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_α), 0.0))

    k_out  = length(out_xs)
    α̂_disp = vcat(α̂[2:k_out+1], [α̂[1]]); se_disp = vcat(se_α[2:k_out+1], [se_α[1]])
    α_lam  = α̂[end]; se_lam = se_α[end]
    z_disp = α̂_disp ./ se_disp
    p_disp = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_disp)))
    crit   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    lo_disp = α̂_disp .- crit .* se_disp; hi_disp = α̂_disp .+ crit .* se_disp
    z_lam = α_lam / se_lam
    p_lam = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z_lam)))
    lo_lam = α_lam - crit * se_lam; hi_lam = α_lam + crit * se_lam

    se_pr = sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(Matrix(GLM.vcov(m_pr))))
    z_pr  = γ̂ ./ se_pr
    p_pr  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_pr)))
    lo_pr = γ̂ .- crit .* se_pr; hi_pr = γ̂ .+ crit .* se_pr
    i_int_pr = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn_pr)
    slope_pr = setdiff(1:length(γ̂), [i_int_pr]); ord_pr = vcat(slope_pr, [i_int_pr])
    cn_pr_ord = vcat(cn_pr[slope_pr], "_cons")
    γ̂_disp = γ̂[ord_pr]; se_pr_disp = se_pr[ord_pr]
    z_pr_disp = z_pr[ord_pr]; p_pr_disp = p_pr[ord_pr]
    lo_pr_disp = lo_pr[ord_pr]; hi_pr_disp = hi_pr[ord_pr]

    βs = α̂_disp[1:end-1]; Vs = V_α[2:k_out+1, 2:k_out+1]
    Wald = try (βs' * (LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(Vs) \ βs))::Float64
           catch; (βs' * LinearAlgebra.pinv(Vs) * βs)::Float64 end
    Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(k_out), Wald)

    if !quiet
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%-48s%-13s = %10s\n",
                       "Heckman selection model -- two-step estimates",
                       "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n_full))
        Printf.@printf("%-48s    %-13s = %10s\n",
                       "(regression model with sample selection)", "Selected",
                       _c13_commafmt(n_pos))
        Printf.@printf("%-48s    %-13s = %10s\n", "", "Nonselected", _c13_commafmt(n_zero))
        println()
        Printf.@printf("%48s%-13s = %10.2f\n", "", "Wald chi2($(k_out))", Wald)
        Printf.@printf("%48s%-13s = %10.4f\n", "", "Prob > chi2", Wald_p)
        println()
        println("-"^78)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                       "", 100*level)
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", depvar_y)
        for i in 1:k_out
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           string(out_xs[i]), _c13_g9(α̂_disp[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se_disp[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_disp[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_disp[i]),
                           _c13_g9(lo_disp[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_disp[i]; w=10))
        end
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       "_cons", _c13_g9(α̂_disp[end]; w=10), _c13_g9(se_disp[end]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_disp[end]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_disp[end]),
                       _c13_g9(lo_disp[end]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_disp[end]; w=10))
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", depvar_d)
        for i in eachindex(γ̂_disp)
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           cn_pr_ord[i], _c13_g9(γ̂_disp[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se_pr_disp[i]; w=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_pr_disp[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_pr_disp[i]),
                           _c13_g9(lo_pr_disp[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_pr_disp[i]; w=10))
        end
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", "/mills")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       "lambda", _c13_g9(α_lam; w=10), _c13_g9(se_lam; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_lam), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_lam),
                       _c13_g9(lo_lam; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_lam; w=10))
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s\n", "rho",   Printf.@sprintf("%9.5f", ρ̂))
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s\n", "sigma", Printf.@sprintf("%9.7f", σ̂))
        println("-"^78)
    end
    return (; β_outcome = α̂_disp, se_outcome = se_disp, V_outcome = V_α,
              β_selection = γ̂, se_selection = se_pr, β_lambda = α_lam,
              se_lambda = se_lam, σ = σ̂, ρ = ρ̂, n = n_full, n_pos, n_zero,
              outcome_coefnames = vcat(string.(out_xs), ["_cons"]),
              selection_coefnames = cn_pr_ord)
end
```

# 13 Bootstrap methods

## 13.1 Introduction

## 13.2 Bootstrap methods

### 13.2.1 Bootstrap estimate of standard error

### 13.2.2 Bootstrap methods

### 13.2.3 Asymptotic refinement

### 13.2.4 Use the bootstrap with caution

## 13.3 Bootstrap pairs

### 13.3.1 Bootstrap-pairs method

### 13.3.2 Bootstrap option

### 13.3.3 Bootstrap standard-errors example

```{julia}
#=
* Sample is only the first 50 observations of chapter 10 data
use musr/mus10data.dta
quietly keep if year02 == 1
quietly drop if _n > 50
quietly keep docvis chronic age
quietly save musr/bootdata, replace
=#
# Use first 50 observations of year02 = 1 from the doctor visits data
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()
@printf("Bootstrap dataset: %d obs\n", DataFrames.nrow(df_boot))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Option vce(bootstrap) to compute bootstrap standard errors
poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots)
=#
# Stata `poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots)`.
# `poisson_boot_stata` does the full Stata-format output: Number of obs /
# Replications / Wald chi2 / Log likelihood / Pseudo R² header, "Observed
# coefficient | Bootstrap std. err. | … | Normal-based CI" subheaders,
# slopes-first / _cons-last row order.
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()
poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); B = 400, seed = 10101);
```

### 13.3.4 How many bootstraps?

```{julia}
#=
* Bootstrap standard errors for different reps and seeds
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(50) seed(10101))
estimates store boot50
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(50) seed(20202))
estimates store boot50diff
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(2000) seed(10101))
estimates store boot2000
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)
estimates store robust
estimates table boot50 boot50diff boot2000 robust, b(%8.5f) se(%8.5f)
=#
# How sensitive are the bootstrap SEs to (a) the number of replications and
# (b) the RNG seed? Compare four estimators of Var(β̂) for the same β̂:
#   boot50      — B = 50,   seed = 10101
#   boot50diff  — B = 50,   seed = 20202
#   boot2000    — B = 2000, seed = 10101
#   robust      — sandwich (no bootstrap)
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

print("running boot50 (B=50, seed=10101)... ")
b50    = poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                            B = 50,   seed = 10101, quiet = true);  println("done")
print("running boot50diff (B=50, seed=20202)... ")
b50d   = poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                            B = 50,   seed = 20202, quiet = true);  println("done")
print("running boot2000 (B=2000, seed=10101)... ")
b2000  = poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                            B = 2000, seed = 10101, quiet = true);  println("done")
rob    = stata_poisson(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                       vce = :robust, quiet = true)

β_disp  = b50.β   # GLM order: [_cons, chronic]
se_b50  = b50.se;  se_b50d = b50d.se;  se_b2k = b2000.se
se_rob  = sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(rob.V_glm))
i_chr = 2; i_con = 1   # GLM puts (Intercept) first

println("\n", "-"^65)
Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s %10s %10s %10s\n",
               "Variable", "boot50", "boot50diff", "boot2000", "robust")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^51)
for (lab, idx) in [("chronic", i_chr), ("_cons", i_con)]
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10.5f %10.5f %10.5f %10.5f\n",
                   lab, β_disp[idx], β_disp[idx], β_disp[idx], β_disp[idx])
    Printf.@printf("%12s | (%8.5f) (%8.5f) (%8.5f) (%8.5f)\n",
                   "", se_b50[idx], se_b50d[idx], se_b2k[idx], se_rob[idx])
end
println("-"^65);
```

### 13.3.5 Clustered bootstraps

```{julia}
#=
* Option vce(boot, cluster) to compute cluster-bootstrap standard errors
poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, cluster(age) reps(400) seed(10101) nodots)

poisson docvis chronic, vce(cluster age)
=#
# (1) Cluster bootstrap: `poisson_boot_stata(... cluster_var = :age)` resamples
#     the unique age groups (panel_bootstrap), refits Poisson on each, and
#     prints "(Replications based on G clusters in age)".
# (2) Analytical cluster-robust SE: `stata_poisson(... vce = :cluster,
#     cluster_var = :age)` uses the CR1 sandwich and prints
#     "(Std. err. adjusted for G clusters in age)".
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                   B = 400, seed = 10101, cluster_var = :age)

println()
stata_poisson(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
              vce = :cluster, cluster_var = :age);
```

### 13.3.6 Bootstrap confidence intervals

### 13.3.7 Postestimation bootstrap functions

### 13.3.8 Bootstrap CI example

```{julia}
# Stata: `poisson docvis chronic, vce(boot, reps(999) seed(10101) bca);
# estat bootstrap, all` — all four bootstrap CI types side-by-side.
# `poisson_boot_ci_stata` does the full pipeline: fit → B=999 obs bootstrap
# reps → leave-one-out jackknife → Observed coef / Bias / Bootstrap SE +
# four CI rows per coef labelled (N), (P), (BC), (BCa).
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

bs_fit = poisson_boot_ci_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                               B = 999, seed = 10101);
```

### 13.3.9 Bootstrap estimate of bias

```{julia}
#=
* List the average of the bootstraps
matrix list e(b_bs)
=#
# Stata's `e(b_bs)` is the mean of the bootstrap β-matrix across replicates,
# stored as a 1×k row matrix with equation-prefixed column names. We
# reproduce the format from the `bs_fit.β_boot` matrix (GLM order
# [(Intercept), chronic]; Stata displays slopes first, then _cons).
β_bs    = vec(Statistics.mean(bs_fit.β_boot, dims = 1))
chronic = β_bs[2]; _cons = β_bs[1]
strip0(s) = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
println()
println("e(b_bs)[1,2]")
println("       docvis:    docvis:")
println("      chronic      _cons")
@printf("y1  %9s  %9s\n",
        strip0(Printf.@sprintf("%.8f", chronic)),
        strip0(Printf.@sprintf("%.8f", _cons)))
```

## 13.4 Bootstrap with custom estimators

### 13.4.1 The bootstrap framework

### 13.4.2 Bootstrap of a parameter estimate

```{julia}
# Stata's `bootstrap, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots noheader: poisson docvis
# chronic` runs the same Poisson + bootstrap pipeline as vce(boot, …), with
# `noheader` suppressing the regression-header block.
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

poisson_boot_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                   B = 400, seed = 10101, noheader = true);
```

### 13.4.3 Bootstrap of a standard error

```{julia}
# `poisson_boot_b_se_stata` bootstraps both β and the OIM SEs in one pass.
# Each resample refits Poisson, saves β̂ AND se(β̂), then prints Stata's
# two-block table:  docvis block (bootstrap of β) and docvis_se block
# (bootstrap of se(β)).
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

poisson_boot_b_se_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic);
                        B = 400, seed = 10101);
```

### 13.4.4 Bootstrap of a user-written estimator

```{julia}
# Stata's `program poissrobust, eclass` just fits Poisson with vce(robust)
# and stores e(b)/e(V). `stata_poisson(... vce = :robust)` returns a
# NamedTuple with β, V, coefnames in Stata-display order (slopes first).
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

# Step 1 — run the wrapper (full Stata-format table)
fit_pr = stata_poisson(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); vce = :robust)

# Step 2 — `ereturn display`: just the coefficient table, capital
# "Std. err." (no "Robust" subheader), with a "docvis" equation banner row.
println("\n. ereturn display")
β    = fit_pr.β; V = fit_pr.V; se = fit_pr.se
crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 0.975)
z_v  = β ./ se
p_v  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_v)))
lo_v = β .- crit .* se; hi_v = β .+ crit .* se

println("-"^78)
println("             | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95% conf. interval]")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", string(GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic).lhs))
for i in eachindex(β)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   fit_pr.coefnames[i], _c13_g9(β[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se[i]; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z_v[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p_v[i]),
                   _c13_g9(lo_v[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi_v[i]; w=10))
end
println("-"^78)
```

### 13.4.5 Bootstrap of a two-step estimator

```{julia}
# Heckman two-step on the mus16 ambulatory-expenditure data:
#   Stage 1: probit of dy = 1{y > 0} on xlist (selection)
#   Stage 2: OLS of ln(y) on xlist + invmills, invmills = φ(x'γ̂)/Φ(x'γ̂)
# `stata_probit` gives the Stata probit table; we build `invmills` from the
# probit linear index, restrict to y > 0, run the outcome regression, then
# print the `ereturn display` block (β only, no SEs).
df16 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus16data.dta"))
df16.y  = Float64.(df16.ambexp)
df16.dy = Int.(df16.y .> 0)
xs      = [:age, :female, :educ, :blhisp, :totchr, :ins]

# Stage 1 — probit on rows with non-missing dy + xs
df_pr = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:dy], xs)])
fml_p = StatsModels.term(:dy) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(xs))
pr_fit = stata_probit(df_pr, fml_p)

# Stage 2 — invmills on the same df_pr rows, then subset to y > 0.
xb = GLM.modelmatrix(pr_fit.model) * pr_fit.β_glm
df_pr.invmills = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb) ./
                 Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb)
df_pr.y = df16[DataFrames.completecases(df16[:, vcat([:dy], xs)]), :y]
df_pos = DataFrames.filter(r -> r.y > 0, df_pr)
df_pos.lny = log.(df_pos.y)

regress_stata(df_pos, :lny, vcat(xs, [:invmills]))

# `ereturn display` — Stata posts only β (no V), so just the Coefficient col.
m_out = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_pos,
    GLM.term(:lny) ~ sum(GLM.term.(vcat(xs, [:invmills]))))
β_out  = StatsBase.coef(m_out)
cn_out = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_out))
i_int  = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn_out)
slope  = setdiff(1:length(β_out), [i_int]); ord = vcat(slope, [i_int])
labels = [cn_out[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn_out[i] for i in ord]

println("\n. ereturn display")
println("-"^78)
println("             | Coefficient")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
for (lab, i) in zip(labels, ord)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", lab, _c13_g9(β_out[i]; w=10))
end
println("-"^78)
```

```{julia}
# Stata `heckman lny $xlist, select(dy = $xlist) twostep` — full Heckman
# (1979) two-step with first-stage-corrected SEs, σ, ρ, λ block via
# `heckman_twostep_stata`.
df16 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus16data.dta"))
df16.y  = Float64.(df16.ambexp)
df16.dy = Int.(df16.y .> 0)
df16.lny = Vector{Union{Missing, Float64}}(missing, DataFrames.nrow(df16))
for i in 1:DataFrames.nrow(df16)
    if Int(df16.dy[i]) == 1
        df16.lny[i] = log(Float64(df16.y[i]))
    end
end

xs      = [:age, :female, :educ, :blhisp, :totchr, :ins]
fml_out = StatsModels.term(:lny) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(xs))
fml_sel = StatsModels.term(:dy)  ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(xs))

heckman_twostep_stata(df16, fml_out, fml_sel; depvar_y = "lny", depvar_d = "dy");
```

### 13.4.6 Bootstrap Hausman test

```{julia}
#=
* Bootstrap for Heckman two-step estimator using chapter 16 example
bootstrap _b, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots nowarn: hecktwostep
=#
# Bootstrap of the full Heckman two-step pipeline. Stata's
# `bootstrap _b: hecktwostep` resamples observations from the FULL
# pre-stage-1 dataset, runs the entire pipeline on each resample, and saves
# the OLS β. We mirror that with `obs_bootstrap` wrapping a closure.
df16 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus16data.dta"))
df16.y  = Float64.(df16.ambexp)
df16.dy = Int.(df16.y .> 0)
xs      = [:age, :female, :educ, :blhisp, :totchr, :ins]
df_h    = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:y, :dy], xs)])
fml_pr  = StatsModels.term(:dy)  ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(xs))
fml_ols = StatsModels.term(:lny) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(vcat(xs, [:invmills])))

function _hecktwostep(d)
    m_pr = GLM.glm(fml_pr, d, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
    xb   = GLM.modelmatrix(m_pr) * GLM.coef(m_pr)
    d2   = DataFrames.copy(d)
    d2.invmills = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb) ./
                  Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb)
    keep = Bool[Int(dy) == 1 for dy in d2.dy]
    d_pos = d2[keep, :]; d_pos.lny = log.(Float64.(d_pos.y))
    return StatsBase.coef(FixedEffectModels.reg(d_pos, fml_ols))
end

β_hat  = _hecktwostep(df_h)
n_full = DataFrames.nrow(df_h)

print("running 400 reps... "); t0 = time()
β_boot = obs_bootstrap(df_h; B = 400, seed = 10101, verbose = false) do d
    _hecktwostep(d)
end
@printf("done in %.2fs\n", time() - t0)

se_b = vec(Statistics.std(β_boot, dims = 1))
crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 0.975)
z    = β_hat ./ se_b
p    = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
lo   = β_hat .- crit .* se_b; hi = β_hat .+ crit .* se_b

cn  = ["(Intercept)", "age", "female", "educ", "blhisp", "totchr", "ins", "invmills"]
ord = vcat(2:length(cn), [1])

println()
Printf.@printf("%-59s%-13s = %5s\n",
               "Bootstrap results", "Number of obs", _c13_commafmt(n_full))
Printf.@printf("%59s%-13s = %5d\n", "", "Replications", 400)
println()
println("-"^78)
println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based")
Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n", "lny")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
for i in ord
    label = cn[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : cn[i]
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   label, _c13_g9(β_hat[i]; w=10), _c13_g9(se_b[i]; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z[i]), Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                   _c13_g9(lo[i]; w=9), _c13_g9(hi[i]; w=10))
end
println("-"^78);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Program to return (b1-b2) for hausman test of endogeneity
program hausmantest, eclass
  version 11
  tempname b bols biv
  regress ldrugexp hi_empunion totchr age female blhisp linc, vce(robust)
  matrix `bols' = e(b)
  ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) totchr age female blhisp linc, vce(robust)
  matrix `biv' = e(b)
  matrix `b' = `bols' - `biv'
  ereturn post `b'
end
=#
# Stata `program hausmantest` runs OLS and 2SLS on the same regressors
# (hi_empunion instrumented by ssiratio), then posts β̂_OLS − β̂_IV as the
# Hausman endogeneity statistic. We compose `regress_stata` and
# `stata_ivregress_2sls`, then print the difference in `ereturn display` style.
df6 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus06data.dta"))
xs6 = [:totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc]

# Stage 1 — OLS with vce(robust); hi_empunion first to match Stata's order.
regress_stata(df6, :ldrugexp, vcat([:hi_empunion], xs6);
              vcov = Vcov.robust(), noheader = true)

# Stage 2 — 2SLS with vce(robust); first = false suppresses the first-stage table.
stata_ivregress_2sls(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion => :ssiratio, xs6;
                     robust = true, first = false)

# Compute β̂_OLS − β̂_IV (refit directly to grab coefficients).
df6c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df6,
    [:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])
m_ols = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c,
    GLM.@formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
    Vcov.robust())
m_iv  = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c,
    GLM.@formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                 (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio)), Vcov.robust())

cn_ols = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_ols)); cn_iv = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_iv))
β_ols  = StatsBase.coef(m_ols); β_iv = StatsBase.coef(m_iv)
labels = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"]
function _lookup(cn, lab)
    target = lab == "_cons" ? "(Intercept)" : lab
    findfirst(==(target), cn)
end
diff_v = [β_ols[_lookup(cn_ols, l)] - β_iv[_lookup(cn_iv, l)] for l in labels]

println("\n. ereturn display")
println("-"^78)
println("             | Coefficient")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
for (lab, d) in zip(labels, diff_v)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", lab, _c13_g9(d; w=10))
end
println("-"^78);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Bootstrap estimates for Hausman test using chapter 6 example
use musr/mus06data.dta, clear
bootstrap _b, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots nowarn: hausmantest
=#
# Bootstrap of the Hausman test β̂_OLS − β̂_IV. Each resample refits both OLS
# and 2SLS, computes the difference vector, and stores it. `obs_bootstrap`
# resamples; `bootstrap_b_table_stata` prints the Stata-format table.
df6 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus06data.dta"))
df6c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df6,
    [:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])

labels = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"]
fml_ols = GLM.@formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc)
fml_iv  = GLM.@formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio))

function _hausmantest(d)
    m_ols = FixedEffectModels.reg(d, fml_ols)
    m_iv  = FixedEffectModels.reg(d, fml_iv)
    cn_ols = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_ols)); cn_iv = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_iv))
    β_ols  = StatsBase.coef(m_ols); β_iv = StatsBase.coef(m_iv)
    function _lookup(cn, lab)
        target = lab == "_cons" ? "(Intercept)" : lab
        findfirst(==(target), cn)
    end
    return [β_ols[_lookup(cn_ols, l)] - β_iv[_lookup(cn_iv, l)] for l in labels]
end

β_obs = _hausmantest(df6c); n_obs = DataFrames.nrow(df6c)

print("running 400 reps... "); t0 = time()
β_boot = obs_bootstrap(df6c; B = 400, seed = 10101, verbose = false) do d
    _hausmantest(d)
end
@printf("done in %.2fs\n", time() - t0)

bootstrap_b_table_stata(β_obs, β_boot, labels; n_obs = n_obs, depvar = "");
```

```{julia}
#=
* Perform Hausman test on the potentially endogenous regressor
test hi_empunion
=#
# Stata's `test hi_empunion` (after `bootstrap _b: hausmantest`) tests
# H0: β̂_OLS_hi_empunion − β̂_IV_hi_empunion = 0 using the bootstrap
# variance. The exported `stata_test` only accepts a model object, so we use
# the small inline `_c13_test_boot` that takes β/V/coefnames directly.
V_boot = Statistics.cov(β_boot)            # k×k bootstrap variance matrix
_c13_test_boot("hi_empunion"; β = β_obs, V = V_boot, coefnames = labels, depvar = "");
```

```{julia}
# Joint Hausman test on all 6 regressors. Pass a vector of variable names;
# `_c13_test_boot` builds R (6×7), computes the joint chi²(6), and prints the
# equation list + chi² block in Stata's format.
_c13_test_boot(["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc"];
               β = β_obs, V = V_boot, coefnames = labels, depvar = "");
```

### 13.4.7 Bootstrap SE of the coefficient of variation

```{julia}
# Bootstrap of a SCALAR statistic — the coefficient of variation
# `coeffvar = std(docvis) / mean(docvis)` — on resamples of the bootdata.
# `obs_bootstrap` resamples; the closure returns a 1-element vector;
# `bootstrap_b_table_stata` formats the output with the "Command:" / named
# expression sub-header.
df_bd = _c13_bootdata()
n_bd  = DataFrames.nrow(df_bd)

function _coeffvar(d)
    y = Float64.(d.docvis)
    return [Statistics.std(y) / Statistics.mean(y)]
end

cv_obs = _coeffvar(df_bd)
print("running 400 reps... "); t0 = time()
cv_boot = obs_bootstrap(df_bd; B = 400, seed = 10101, verbose = false) do d
    _coeffvar(d)
end
@printf("done in %.2fs\n", time() - t0)

# Persist the 400 replicates (Stata's `saving(musr/coeffofvar, replace)`).
mkpath("musr")
CSV.write("musr/coeffofvar.csv", DataFrames.DataFrame(coeffvar = vec(cv_boot)))

bootstrap_b_table_stata(cv_obs, cv_boot, ["coeffvar"];
                        n_obs = n_bd, depvar = "",
                        command = "summarize docvis",
                        expressions = ["coeffvar" => "r(sd)/r(mean)"]);
```

## 13.5 Bootstraps with asymptotic refinement

### 13.5.1 Percentile-t method

### 13.5.2 Percentile-t Wald test

```{julia}
#=
* Percentile-t for a single coefficient: Bootstrap the t statistic
use musr/bootdata, clear
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)
local theta = _b[chronic]
local setheta = _se[chronic]
bootstrap tstar=((_b[chronic]-`theta')/_se[chronic]), seed(10101)        ///
  reps(999) nodots saving(musr/percentilet, replace): poisson docvis chronic, ///
  vce(robust)
=#
# Percentile-t bootstrap for β_chronic via `bootstrap_tstar_stata`:
#   1. Fit Poisson, vce(robust) → (θ, σ_θ)
#   2. Bootstrap t* = (β̂*_chronic − θ)/SE*_chronic over B = 999 resamples
#   3. Persist replicates to musr/percentilet.csv
#   4. Print the Stata `bootstrap … : poisson` table
df_boot = _c13_bootdata()

ptres = bootstrap_tstar_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic), :chronic;
                              B = 999, seed = 10101, vce = :robust,
                              command = "poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)",
                              save_csv = "musr/percentilet.csv")
β_orig  = ptres.β_orig; se_orig = ptres.se_orig; tstars = ptres.tstars
```

```{julia}
#=
* Percentile-t p-value for symmetric two-sided Wald test of H0: theta = 0
use musr/percentilet, clear
quietly count if abs(`theta'/`setheta') < abs(tstar)
display "p-value = " r(N)/_N
=#
# Percentile-t two-sided Wald test of H0: θ = 0 from the bootstrap t*
# distribution:  p = (1/B)·Σ 1{|t*_b| > |t_obs|},  t_obs = β_orig/se_orig.
percentilet_pvalue_stata(tstars, β_orig, se_orig);
```

### 13.5.3 Percentile-t CI

```{julia}
#=
* Percentile-t critical values and confidence interval
_pctile tstar, p(2.5,97.5)
scalar lb = `theta' + r(r1)*`setheta'
scalar ub = `theta' + r(r2)*`setheta'
display "2.5 and 97.5 percentiles of t* distn: " r(r1) ", " r(r2) _n ///
    "95 percent percentile-t confidence interval is  (" lb ","  ub ")"
=#
# Percentile-t 95% CI from the t* distribution and (β_orig, se_orig):
#   [β_orig + q(α/2)·se_orig,  β_orig + q(1-α/2)·se_orig].
percentilet_ci_stata(tstars, β_orig, se_orig);
```

```{julia}
#=
use musr/bootdata, clear
nbreg docvis chronic
=#
# `stata_nbreg` (ch12) fits NB2 jointly in (β, lnα), prints the Stata header
# (LR chi2 / Pseudo R²), the coefficient table with /lnalpha and alpha rows,
# and the LR test of α=0 reported as chibar2(01).
nb_chronic = stata_nbreg(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic));
```

## 13.6 Bootstrap pairs via custom resampling

### 13.6.1 Manual resampling helper

### 13.6.2 Custom bootstrap loop

```{julia}
#=
* Program to do one bootstrap replication
program onebootrep, rclass
  version 11
  drop _all
  use musr/bootdata
  bsample
  poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)
  return scalar tstar = (_b[chronic]-$theta)/_se[chronic]
end

* Now do 999 bootstrap replications
use musr/bootdata, clear
quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)
global theta = _b[chronic]
global setheta = _se[chronic]
simulate tstar=r(tstar), seed(10101) reps(999) nodots  ///
  saving(musr/percentilet2, replace): onebootrep

* Analyze the results to get the p-value
use musr/percentilet2, clear
quietly count if abs($theta/$setheta) < abs(tstar)
display "p-value = " r(N)/_N
=#
# Same percentile-t pipeline as §13.5.2 but via Stata's `simulate`.
# `bootstrap_tstar_stata` returns the t* distribution;
# `percentilet_pvalue_stata` computes the symmetric two-sided p-value.
ptres2 = bootstrap_tstar_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic), :chronic;
                               B = 999, seed = 10101, vce = :robust,
                               command = "poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)",
                               save_csv = "musr/percentilet2.csv", quiet = true)
percentilet_pvalue_stata(ptres2.tstars, ptres2.β_orig, ptres2.se_orig);
```

### 13.6.3 Bootstrap Monte Carlo exercise

```{julia}
#=
* Program to do one bootstrap of B replications
program mybootstrap, rclass
  use musr/bootdata, clear
  quietly poisson docvis chronic, vce(robust)
  global theta = _b[chronic]
  global setheta = _se[chronic]
  simulate tstar=r(tstar), reps(999) nodots  ///
    saving(musr/percentilet2, replace): onebootrep
  use musr/percentilet2, clear
  quietly count if abs($theta/$setheta) < abs(tstar)
  return scalar pvalue =  r(N)/_N
end

* Check the program by running once
set seed 10101
mybootstrap
display r(pvalue)
=#
# `mybootstrap_stata` wraps the full §13.6.2 percentile-t pipeline into one
# call returning the symmetric two-sided Wald p-value. Re-runnable with
# different seeds. Mirrors `set seed 10101; mybootstrap; display r(pvalue)`.
mb_one = mybootstrap_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic), :chronic;
                            B = 999, seed = 10101, vce = :robust);
```

## 13.7 Alternative resampling schemes

### 13.7.1 Bootstrap pairs

```{julia}
#=
* Program to resample using bootstrap pairs
program bootpairs
  version 11
  drop _all
  use musr/bootdata
  bsample
  poisson docvis chronic
end

* Check the program by running once
bootpairs
=#
# `stata_bsample` is Stata's `bsample` — observation-level resample with
# replacement. `stata_poisson` with vce=:oim matches the default
# `poisson docvis chronic` (OIM SEs). RNG note: Julia's MersenneTwister
# differs from Stata's MT64, so the resampled rows and coefficients will not
# match Stata's exact "one run" output.
poisson_out = stata_poisson(stata_bsample(df_boot; seed = 10101),
                            GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); vce = :oim);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Bootstrap pairs for the parameters
simulate _b, seed(10101) reps(400) nodots: bootpairs
summarize
=#
# `simulate _b, reps(400): bootpairs` runs the bootstrap-pair program 400
# times saving the coefficient vector each time, then `summarize` reports
# univariate stats for _b_chronic / _b_cons. `obs_bootstrap` does the loop;
# `stata_summarize` prints the summary table. RNG note: MersenneTwister ≠
# Stata's MT64, so summary statistics differ; means should be near the
# full-sample β.
β_pairs_mat = obs_bootstrap(df_boot; B = 400, seed = 10101, verbose = false) do d
    fit = stata_poisson(d, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); vce = :oim, quiet = true)
    return [fit.β_glm[findfirst(==("chronic"),     fit.coefnames_glm)],
            fit.β_glm[findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), fit.coefnames_glm)]]
end

df_pairs = DataFrames.DataFrame(:_b_chronic => β_pairs_mat[:, 1],
                                :_b_cons    => β_pairs_mat[:, 2])
stata_summarize(df_pairs);
```

```{julia}
#=
// Following not included in book
* Compare to bootstrap command
use musr/bootdata, clear
bootstrap, reps(100) seed(10101): poisson docvis chronic
=#
# Stata's `bootstrap _b: poisson docvis chronic` — observation-level
# bootstrap of the Poisson coefficients. `obs_bootstrap` does the loop
# returning [chronic, _cons] per replicate; `bootstrap_b_table_stata` prints
# the Stata header and Normal-based CI table. Numbers differ due to the RNG.
fit0_b = stata_poisson(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); vce = :oim, quiet = true)
i_chr  = findfirst(==("chronic"),     fit0_b.coefnames_glm)
i_cons = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), fit0_b.coefnames_glm)

β_boot100 = obs_bootstrap(df_boot; B = 100, seed = 10101, verbose = false) do d
    fit = stata_poisson(d, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); vce = :oim, quiet = true)
    return [fit.β_glm[findfirst(==("chronic"),     fit.coefnames_glm)],
            fit.β_glm[findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), fit.coefnames_glm)]]
end

bootstrap_b_table_stata([fit0_b.β_glm[i_chr], fit0_b.β_glm[i_cons]],
                        β_boot100, ["chronic", "_cons"];
                        n_obs = DataFrames.nrow(df_boot), depvar = "docvis",
                        command = "poisson docvis chronic");
```

### 13.7.2 Parametric bootstrap

```{julia}
#=
* Estimate the model with original actual data and save estimates
use musr/bootdata
nbreg docvis chronic
predict muhat
global alpha = e(alpha)
=#
# Original-data NB fit and saved quantities (μ̂, α) for the parametric
# bootstrap. `stata_nbreg` (ch12) fits NB2 jointly in (β, lnα).
nb_boot = stata_nbreg(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic))

# Stata `predict muhat` after nbreg — conditional mean μ̂_i = exp(x_iᵀβ̂).
muhat = exp.(GLM.modelmatrix(nb_boot.model) * nb_boot.β_glm)
alpha = nb_boot.α
@printf("alpha = %.6f   mean(muhat) = %.6f\n", alpha, Statistics.mean(muhat))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Program for parametric bootstrap generating from negative binomial
program bootparametric, eclass
  version 11
  capture drop nu dvhat
  generate nu = rgamma(1/$alpha,$alpha)
  generate dvhat = rpoisson(muhat*nu)
  nbreg dvhat chronic
end

// Following not included in book
* Check the program by running once
set seed 10101
bootparametric
=#
# `bootparametric_nb_stata` draws ν ~ Gamma(1/α, α), y* ~ Poisson(μ̂·ν),
# overwrites `dvhat`, and refits NB2 of dvhat on chronic — in one call.
# Mirrors `set seed 10101; bootparametric`.
nb_par_one = bootparametric_nb_stata(df_boot, muhat, alpha,
                                      GLM.@formula(dvhat ~ chronic); seed = 10101);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Parametric bootstrap for the parameters
simulate _b, seed(10101) reps(400) nodots: bootparametric
summarize
=#
# Runs the parametric NB2 program 400 times saving the coefficient vector
# each rep, then summarizes. Seed the global RNG once and let
# `bootparametric_nb_stata` advance it; collect (β_chronic, β_cons, lnα).
# RNG note: MersenneTwister ≠ Stata's MT64, so summary stats differ.
Random.seed!(10101)
β_par_mat = reduce(hcat, [
    let f = bootparametric_nb_stata(df_boot, muhat, alpha,
                                     GLM.@formula(dvhat ~ chronic); quiet = true)
        [f.β_glm[findfirst(==("chronic"),     f.coefnames_glm)],
         f.β_glm[findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), f.coefnames_glm)],
         f.lnα]
    end
    for _ in 1:400])'

df_par = DataFrames.DataFrame(:_b_chronic  => β_par_mat[:, 1],
                              :_b_cons     => β_par_mat[:, 2],
                              :_b_lnalpha  => β_par_mat[:, 3])
stata_summarize(df_par);
```

### 13.7.3 Residual bootstrap

```{julia}
#=
* Program for residual bootstrap for OLS with iid errors
use musr/bootdata, clear
quietly regress docvis chronic
predict uhat, resid
keep uhat
save musr/residuals, replace
program bootresidual
  version 11
  drop _all
  use musr/residuals
  bsample
  merge using musr/bootdata
  regress docvis chronic
  predict xb
  generate ystar =  xb + uhat
  regress ystar chronic
end

// Following not included in book
* Check the program by running once
bootresidual
=#
# `bootresidual_ols_stata` mirrors Stata's `bootresidual`: fits OLS on
# bootdata (table 1), resamples residuals, builds y* = X β̂ + u*, refits y*
# on chronic (table 2), and prints both `regress` tables plus the
# `(option xb assumed; fitted values)` notice. RNG note: the original-data
# table matches Stata exactly; the bootstrap table differs (MersenneTwister
# ≠ MT64).
bootresidual_ols_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); seed = 10101);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Residual bootstrap for the parameters
simulate _b, seed(10101) reps(400) nodots: bootresidual
summarize
=#
# Runs the residual-bootstrap program 400 times saving β̂ each rep, then
# summarizes. Seed the global RNG once and let `bootresidual_ols_stata`
# advance it. RNG note: summary stats won't match Stata exactly.
Random.seed!(10101)
β_res_mat = reduce(hcat, [
    let res = bootresidual_ols_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); quiet = true)
        cn = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(res.boot)); β = FixedEffectModels.coef(res.boot)
        [β[findfirst(==("chronic"),     cn)], β[findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)]]
    end
    for _ in 1:400])'

df_res = DataFrames.DataFrame(:_b_chronic => β_res_mat[:, 1],
                              :_b_cons    => β_res_mat[:, 2])
stata_summarize(df_res);
```

### 13.7.4 Wild bootstrap

```{julia}
#=
* Wild bootstrap for OLS with iid errors
use musr/bootdata, clear
program bootwild
  version 11
  drop _all
  use musr/bootdata
  regress docvis chronic
  predict xb
  predict u, resid
  gen ustar = -0.618034*u
  replace ustar = 1.618034*u if runiform() > 0.723607
  gen ystar =  xb + ustar
  regress ystar chronic
end

// Following not included in book
* Check the program by running once
bootwild
=#
# `bootwild_ols_stata` mirrors Stata's `bootwild`: fits OLS on bootdata
# (table 1), draws Mammen-weighted η_i ∈ {-0.618, 1.618} (P(b) ≈ 0.276),
# forms u* = η·û and y* = X β̂ + u*, refits OLS on y* (table 2). Prints both
# `regress` tables, the fitted-values notice, and the `(<n> real changes
# made)` line. RNG note: table 1 matches Stata; table 2 differs.
bootwild_ols_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); seed = 10101);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Wild bootstrap for the parameters
simulate _b, seed(10101) reps(400) nodots: bootwild
summarize
=#
# Runs the wild-bootstrap program 400 times saving β̂ each rep, then
# summarizes. Seed the global RNG once and let `bootwild_ols_stata` advance
# it. RNG note: summary stats won't match Stata exactly.
Random.seed!(10101)
β_wild_mat = reduce(hcat, [
    let res = bootwild_ols_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic); quiet = true)
        cn = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(res.boot)); β = FixedEffectModels.coef(res.boot)
        [β[findfirst(==("chronic"),     cn)], β[findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)]]
    end
    for _ in 1:400])'

df_wild = DataFrames.DataFrame(:_b_chronic => β_wild_mat[:, 1],
                               :_b_cons    => β_wild_mat[:, 2])
stata_summarize(df_wild);
```

### 13.7.5 Subsampling

## 13.8 The jackknife

### 13.8.1 Jackknife method

### 13.8.2 Jackknife in Julia

```{julia}
#=
* Jackknife estimate of standard errors
use musr/bootdata, replace
poisson docvis chronic, vce(jackknife, mse nodots)
=#
# `poisson_jackknife_stata` computes Stata's `vce(jackknife, mse)` SE:
# refits Poisson on each leave-one-out subset, then
#   V_jack = (n-1)/n · Σ (β̂_(i) − β̂)(β̂_(i) − β̂)ᵀ
# (centered on β̂ because of `mse`). Inference uses t with df = n − 1; the
# omnibus stat is F(k, n − 1).
poisson_jackknife_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic));
```

```{julia}
#=
// Following not included in book
jacknife, mse nodots: poisson docvis chronic
=#
# Same as the previous chunk: the prefix-style `jackknife, mse: poisson` is
# equivalent to `poisson ..., vce(jackknife, mse)`.
poisson_jackknife_stata(df_boot, GLM.@formula(docvis ~ chronic));
```

## 13.9 Stata resources

## 13.10 Exercises
